title: /kernel
summary_text: `/kernel` here means the Kernel sovereign path, not the active FUGUE runtime. The authority split is explicit in [AGENTS.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/AGENTS.md), and the Kernel target state is defined in [docs/requirements-gpt54-codex-kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/requirements-gpt54-codex-kernel.md) and [docs/kernel/requirements-freeze-v1.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/kernel/requirements-freeze-v1.md). The local launcher is [scripts/local/launchers/kernel](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/kernel), which routes into [scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh). I checked the repo’s read-only Kernel guard surface. As of `2026-04-02`, the newest `/kernel` run is stale and invalid, not healthy: - `run_id=chat:MacBook-Pro-M5-5:fugue-orchestrator-public:slash-kernel:20260402T104253:2599` - lifecycle: `blocked` - state: `invalid` - reason: `degraded-specialist-count-insufficient` - blocking reason in detail: `glm-provider-evidence-missing` So I can’t honestly claim “Kernel orchestration is active” for this session from the current runtime evidence. Next step depends on intent: 1. If you want actual Kernel runtime recovery, I’ll debug and repair the guard/bootstrap path. 2. If you want to work in Kernel design mode, I’ll continue using the Kernel docs and contracts without pretending the live bootstrap succeeded.
